    Home › Aircraft › Antonov An-22 Antei

    Antonov An-22 Antei

    Antonov · Cargo · Retired

    The largest turboprop aircraft in history.

    Length57.92 m
    Wingspan64.4 m
    Range5,000 km
    MTOW250 t
    Built68
    First flight1965
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by aircraft.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Range per tonne#64 of 65
    20.0 km/t maximum range ÷ MTOW in tonnes
    Span-to-length ratio#10 of 68
    1.11 wingspan ÷ fuselage length
    Weight per metre#19 of 68
    4.32 t/m MTOW ÷ fuselage length
    Range in hours#33 of 65
    6.8 h maximum range ÷ cruise speed
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The Antonov An-22 Antei's wingspan, against things you already know the size of.

    Antonov An-22 Antei64.4 m
    Football pitch (width)68 m
    Blue whale25 m
    New Routemaster bus11.2 m
    You (probably)1.75 m
    #21 of 68 by length → #18 by weight → #59 by speed →
    Specification

    The numbers

    Length57.92 m
    Wingspan64.40 m
    Height12.53 m
    MTOW250,000 kg
    Max payload60,000 kg
    Engines4 × Kuznetsov NK-12MA (contra-rotating)
    First flight27 February 1965
    Built68

    Compare it

    Put the Antonov An-22 next to something

    vs Airbus Beluga →vs Antonov An-124 →
    vs Antonov An-124 vs Tupolev Tu-114 Compare with anything →

    Last verified: 2026-07-14 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    When the An-22 appeared at the 1965 Paris Air Show, unannounced, the West was stunned: a 250-tonne aircraft — on propellers. Four NK-12 engines, the most powerful turboprops ever made, each spinning two contra-rotating propellers, dragged 60-tonne payloads out of dirt strips no jet could use.

    Named Antei after the giant of Greek myth, it set more than forty world records, carried tanks, missiles and drilling rigs across the Soviet Union, and pioneered the twin-tail, ramp-loading formula the An-124 and An-225 later perfected.

    Sixty-eight were built. Antonov Airlines flew the world's last one commercially into the 2020s before retiring it — the end of the giant turboprop era.

    Common questions

    Antonov An-22 Antei — the short answers

    How fast is the Antonov An-22 Antei?
    The Antonov An-22 Antei cruises at about 740 km/h — roughly 400 knots, or 460 mph.
    How far can the Antonov An-22 Antei fly?
    The Antonov An-22 Antei has a maximum range of about 5,000 km (2,700 nautical miles, 3,108 miles). At its cruise speed that is roughly 6.8 hours in the air.
    Is the Antonov An-22 Antei still in production?
    Status: Retired. The Antonov An-22 Antei first flew in 1965, and about 68 have been built.
    How big is the Antonov An-22 Antei?
    The Antonov An-22 Antei is 57.92 m long with a wingspan of 64.4 m and a height of 12.53 m. Its maximum takeoff weight is 250,000 kg.
    Variants

    The Antonov An-22 Antei line

    An-22
    The production airlifter.
    An-22A
    Uprated later batch.
    Operators

    Who flies the Antonov An-22 Antei

    No current operators — this aircraft's flying days are over. The operators of record are below.

    RUSoviet / Russian Air ForceThe main military fleet across five decades.—
    UAAntonov AirlinesFlew the last airworthy An-22 commercially until its retirement.—

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
